questions.question: Explain the three methods of amortized analysis: Aggregate Analysis, Accounting Method, and Potential Method. Provide one example data structure where amortized analysis is applicable and briefly explain why worst-case analysis would be misleading for that structure.
keyPoints: Aggregate Analysis computes total cost of n operations and divides by n to get amortized cost
Accounting Method (Banker's Method) assigns varying charges to each operation, storing excess as prepaid work
Potential Method (Physicist's Method) defines a potential function measuring stored work in data structure
Example: Dynamic array insertion - worst-case is O(n) but amortized is O(1) per operation

questions.question: Consider a stack that supports the following operations: PUSH (cost 1), POP (cost 1), and MULTIPOP(k) that pops k elements or empties the stack whichever comes first. Starting with an empty stack, perform n operations consisting of n/2 PUSHes and n/2 MULTIPOPs. Using aggregate analysis, show that the total cost is O(n) and determine the amortized cost per operation.
keyPoints: Each element can be pushed at most once and popped at most once per cycle
Total number of POPs across all MULTIPOP operations is at most total number of PUSHes
Aggregate cost for n operations is at most 2n = O(n)
Amortized cost per operation = O(n)/n = O(1)

questions.question: (a) A binary counter stores an integer using an array of bits A[0..k-1] where bit i represents 2^i. The INCREMENT operation starts from the least significant bit and flips bits from 0 to 1, and when a bit flips from 1 to 0, it carries to the next bit. Starting from 0, perform INCREMENT n times. Using aggregate analysis, prove that the total number of bit flips is at most 2n, hence amortized cost is O(1). (b) Using the accounting method, assign amortized costs to each operation (setting bits from 0 to 1 and clearing bits from 1 to 0) and show the amortized cost per INCREMENT is 2. (c) Define a potential function Φ(i) = number of 1-bits in the counter after i INCREMENTs and using the potential method, prove that the amortized cost is O(1).
keyPoints: Aggregate: Bit i toggles every 2^i operations; total toggles ≤ n(1/2 + 1/4 + 1/8 + ...) < 2n
Accounting: Charge 2 for each 0→1 (store credit for future 1→0); charge 0 for 1→0 (use stored credit)
Potential: Φ(i) = number of 1s; amortized cost = c_i + Φ(i) - Φ(i-1) ≤ 2
All three methods yield constant amortized cost O(1) per INCREMENT operation

questions.question: A dynamic array A with capacity c starts with capacity 1 and doubles its capacity whenever it becomes full (i.e., when size = capacity). Analyze the cost of inserting n elements using: (a) Aggregate Analysis: Show total cost is O(n) by summing geometric series costs of expansions. (b) Accounting Method: Assign amortized costs of 1 for regular insert, 3 for insert that triggers expansion, and determine the amortized cost per operation. (c) Potential Method: Define potential function Φ = 2·size - capacity (or 2·size - next_capacity) and prove that amortized cost per insert is O(1). Explain why this shows that inserting n elements costs O(n) time despite occasional O(n) expansions.
keyPoints: Aggregate: Expansion costs form series 1 + 2 + 4 + ... + n/2 + n = 2n - 1 = O(n)
Accounting: Charge 3 per insert; 1 for current element, 2 stored for future copy cost; total 3n
Potential: Φ = 2·size - capacity; when doubling, Φ decreases by 1, offsetting high copy cost
Amortized cost = actual cost + ΔΦ ≤ 3 for all cases, proving total O(n) for n inserts
